content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 4, 2018

Josh Fryday
Mayor, City of Novato
38 Arnold Drive
Novato, CA 94949

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-18-127

Dear Mr. Fryday:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict
of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as
common law conflict of interest or Section 1090. Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when
rendering advice (or Ocletsky (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your
facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions
should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

QUESTIONS

1. Under the Act, may you participate in Novato City Council development decisions
regarding the Hamilton Commissary Parcel and Christmas Tree Hill Parcel?

2. Under the Act, may you participate in decisions related to the location of a transitional
housing project on the Hamilton Housing Parcel?

CONCLUSIONS

1 & 2. Yes, you may participate in these decisions. The facts presented do not indicate that
either of the decisions at issue would have a reasonably foreseeable and material financial effect on
your residential property.

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A -18-127
Page No. 2

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTOR

You are mayor of the City of Novato. The City owns parcels located at the former Hamilton
Army Airfield, a decommissioned military installation ("Hamilton"). The parcels at issue are the
Commissary Parcel, the Christmas Tree Hill Parcel, and the Housing Parcel. You own and reside on
property located in the Hamilton area of the city. Previously we advised that you may participate in
decisions relating to the disposition of three Hamilton parcels, one of which is at issue here.² You
were advised to seek further advice on future development decisions regarding specific parcels.
You have provided the following facts, with additional information provided by your counsel,
Veronica Nebb, by phone regarding the latest Hamilton Parcel decisions.

Development Decisions: Commissary Parcel and Christmas Tree Hill Parcel

The City Council is now considering three proposals for development of the Commissary
Parcel. Currently the three proposals include multi-story, multifamily residential units numbering
from 124 to 185, in the form of townhomes, assisted living and one with an option of a hotel
facility. As of this date none of the proposals have opted to include a proposal for the Christmas
Tree Parcel. The land use designation and zoning for the Commissary Parcel is currently General
Commercial with Planned District Zoning.

The City Council will review and select one or more proposals to proceed to a public
process, and, after public input, the City Council will likely choose a proposed developer from
among the proposals. Any proposals for development will be subject to environmental review,
public hearings before the Planning Commission, the City's Design Review Commission, and
ultimately the City Council's approval. You wish to know if you may participate in each stage of
this development process: review and selection of proposals, negotiating the Exclusive Negotiating
Agreement with the chosen developer, approval of the environmental review, a purchase and sale
agreement or lease with the developer, and approval of any land use entitlements which may be
proposed by the developer.

The Commissary Parcel consists of 4.7 acres located 2,791 feet from your residential
property. The parcel is separated from your property by a housing development under construction,
the NWP railroad, Hamilton Parkway, two large housing developments (235 homes, mature
landscaping and sound-walls), and 32 acres of open space. Because various structures and land
forms (the slopes of hills) lie between your residence and this parcel, you do not believe that any
structures resulting from the development of the parcel will be visible from your residence. The
Commissary Parcel's nearest access road, State Access Road, is separate from the Hamilton
Parkway access that you utilize to reach your home. The Christmas Tree Hill Parcel is a 2.48-acre
parcel containing a very large rock outcropping located between Highway 101 and the Commissary
Parcel. Per the City Ordinance, the outcropping cannot be graded, however, the City is open to
creative reuses of the site that will benefit the redeveloped Commissary Parcel and the community
at large. The rock outcropping has been used in the past for a Christmas tree display; however, none
of the three proposals currently include proposals for the Christmas Tree Hill Parcel.

² Fryday Advice Letter No. A-16-087.

File No. A -18-127
Page No. 3

Previously, we determined that City decisions regarding the Commissary Parcel's
disposition alone would not foreseeably and materially affect your financial interest in your
residential real property. We noted that the distance of the parcel from your residence, developed
nature of the area, intervening structures and hill slopes indicated that the disposition of the parcel
would not be likely to change the character of the property or otherwise affect its market value.

Transitional Housing Project- Housing Parcel

Homeward Bound of Marin has proposed a transitional housing project for veterans on the
Hamilton Housing Parcel. This parcel is located near the Commissary Parcel and is approximately
2,544 feet from your residential property. The Housing Parcel is immediately adjacent to the current
Homeward Bound homeless shelter and transitional housing units, and affiliated Next Key training
center at Hamilton. No clear specifics are currently available regarding this request except that
Homeward Bound is interested in pursuing an expansion of their services to include the
construction and operation of new transitional rental housing units to serve veterans on the Housing
Parcel.

The Housing Parcel was acquired by the City from the federal government as part of the
reuse actions for Hamilton and has, since its acquisition in the 1990s, been planned for additional
homeless service uses pursuant to the approved Reuse Plan for Hamilton. The parcel currently has
several warehouses, most of which are abandoned and one of which is temporarily used by a
homeless service organization for storage and meetings. The Housing Parcel's parking area is
currently a mobile shower facility for the homeless, with some limited parking.

Your residence is separated from the Housing Parcel by the same features that separate your
residence from the Commissary Parcel. Because of these intervening structures and properties, as
well of as the overall distance of over 2,500 feet and the sloping topography, you state that the
Housing Parcel cannot be seen from your residence. State Access Road similarly provides the
closest access to the Housing Parcel. You wish to know if you may participate in preliminary
community meetings and decisions as well as later City Council decisions on the disposition of the
Housing Parcel, and any necessary entitlements regarding this proposal.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest.
(Section 87103.) A conflict of interest may arise only when the reasonably foreseeable financial
effect of a governmental decision on a public official's interests is material. Different standards
apply to determine whether a reasonably foreseeable financial effect on an interest will be material
depending on the nature of the interest. Interests from which a conflict of interest may arise are
defined in Section 87103. A real property interest is the only financial interest implicated by your
facts. Similar to your previous advice letter, our analysis regarding the Commissary, Christmas Tree
Hill and Housing Parcels is limited to the facts presented, the decisions presented, and the financial
interest identified.

File No. A -18-127
Page No. 4

Foreseeability & Materiality

Pursuant to Regulation 18701, your residence is not explicitly involved in the decisions at
issue and a financial effect will be reasonably foreseeable if it is a realistic possibility and more than
hypothetical. Under the applicable materiality standards for real property set forth in regulation
18702.2(a), the financial effect is deemed material, if the decisions:

(10) Would change the character of the parcel of real property by substantially
altering traffic levels or intensity of use, including parking, of property
surrounding the official's real property parcel, the view, privacy, noise levels, or
air quality, including odors, or any other factors that would affect the market
value of the real property parcel in which the official has a financial interest.

(12) Would cause a reasonably prudent person, using due care and consideration
under the circumstances, to believe that the governmental decision was of such a
nature that it reasonably foreseeable effect would influence the market value of
the official's property.³

Question 1: Development Decisions: Commissary Parcel and Christmas Tree Hill Parcel

Due to your property's distance from the Commissary Parcel, intervening structures,
development and hill slopes, it does not appear that any of the proposals of less than 200 residential
housing-type units on the Parcel would change the character of your residential parcel or influence
the market value of your home. The Christmas Tree Hill Parcel is similarly distanced from your
residential property; however, none of the proposals include development plans for this parcel.

Question 2: Transitional Housing Project- Housing Parcel

The Housing Parcel is similarly distanced from your residence, and the use anticipated in the
proposal is in keeping with the parcel's current use related to homeless services. No facts are
presented which indicate that decisions related to this proposal for transitional rental housing units
would change the character of your residential parcel or influence the market value of your home.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Brian G. Lau
Assistant General Counsel

By: L. Karen Harrison
Counsel, Legal Division

LKH:jgl

³ This regulation was misidentified in the previous Advice Letter.
